blurb:
Rreal Tacos is one of those places the neighborhood keeps to itself but probably should not. The vibe is loud and fun, the service is attentive, and the food actually lives up to what people say about it. Great for a group dinner or a casual night out when you want good food and a couple of good drinks.

Start with the Esquite before anything else. For drinks, the Pineapple Guava pitcher and the Passion Fruit pitcher are both worth ordering, especially if you are sharing with the table. They are refreshing and well-balanced without being too sweet.

They are open until midnight every night except Sunday, so it is also a solid late-night option if you need one.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
This one is a real hidden gem on Allgood Road. It's a Mexican grocery store with a taqueria in the back, and the food coming out of that kitchen is genuinely excellent. Don't let the setup fool you. The people who know about it keep coming back.

The al pastor is the move here. It's well seasoned, not too crispy, and cooked exactly right. The bean and cheese pupusas are 10 out of 10, and the chicken plate with rice and beans is worth ordering too. The beans are made from scratch, not from a can, and you can tell. The tortas are great as well if you want something handheld.

This is a cash-and-carry kind of spot, cheap and fast. Perfect for lunch or a quick weeknight dinner when you want real food without a big bill.
address: 786 Allgood Rd NE, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Laredos is our go-to on Sandy Plains when we want solid Mexican food without a lot of fuss. The inside is colorful and festive, and it has that cantina feel that makes it work for a family dinner or a casual night out with friends. The margaritas are a big draw and worth ordering.

They do all the Mexican classics well. It's the kind of place where everyone at the table finds something they want, and the portions are generous for the price. Good for a weeknight when you don't want to cook but also don't want to spend a lot.

They're open every day until at least 10pm, which is handy when the dinner decision happens late.
address: 1860 Sandy Plains Rd, Marietta, GA 30066, USA

blurb:
This is the place we pull out for anniversaries and birthdays when we want it to actually feel special. It sits right on the Chattahoochee River off Powers Ferry, and the setting really does the heavy lifting. The view alone makes it worth the drive.

The menu leans into Southern seafood, so expect that kind of cooking done well. It's on the pricier side, but for a date night or a celebratory dinner, it hits the mark. Sunday brunch is also popular if you want a more relaxed way to experience it.

Reservations are a good idea, especially on weekends.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
If you've never had Brazilian-style açaí, this little place on Roswell Road is a great introduction. It's a self-serve setup where you build your own bowl and load it up however you want. The staff is friendly and the vibe is casual and relaxed. Good for an afternoon snack or something lighter after a workout.

Beyond the açaí bowls, they also have crepes and coxinha, which is a traditional Brazilian snack. It's a fun spot to try something a little different without going far from home.

They're closed Tuesdays but open until 10pm the rest of the week, which is handy if you want a sweet treat after dinner.
address: 2145 Roswell Rd Suite 110, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Canton Cooks is right on Roswell Road and it's a go-to for Chinese food in the area when you want something a step above the usual takeout routine. The menu goes beyond the standard dishes and includes things like hot pot, BBQ duck, and lobster in black bean sauce. It's a fun spot for a group dinner when everyone wants to try a few different things.

The BBQ duck and lobster in black bean sauce are the standout options if you want to order something a little more adventurous. It's also a good pick for a casual weeknight dinner since they're open until 10pm every day.

Parking and access are straightforward off Roswell Road, and with 800-plus reviews at four stars, it's clearly a neighborhood staple.
address: 5984 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This tiny spot over on Akers Mill Road has a serious following and for good reason. It's no-frills, one communal table, the kind of place where you order at the counter and just eat. The BBQ has a Korean-influenced twist that makes it stand out from every other barbecue spot in the area. Good for a casual lunch or an early dinner.

The menu is small and focused, which is usually a good sign. People come back regularly and the 4.7-star rating across nearly 3,000 reviews tells you this is not a fluke.

They're only open Tuesday through Saturday, 11am to 8pm, and closed Sunday and Monday. It fills up, so don't wait too long in the afternoon.
address: 2243 Akers Mill Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
This is a solid go-to for Mexican food right on Roswell Road. The kitchen puts out good, reliable food and it's the kind of place you can bring the family on a weeknight without a reservation. Open every day and late enough to work for a dinner run after a busy evening.

The food is the reason people keep coming back. Just go in knowing that the margaritas are on the pricier side compared to other spots nearby. Some regulars say the drink prices have gotten steep, so you might want to stick to food or budget accordingly if you're planning a round of drinks.

Good for casual family dinners or a quick lunch.
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
If you need a solid breakfast or lunch spot in Sandy Springs, J. Christopher's is the kind of place you can count on. It's an all-day breakfast and lunch situation, open every day from 7am to 2pm, so it works great for a lazy weekend morning or a quick weekday bite before the day gets busy. The vibe is casual and comfortable, nothing fancy.

They do classic breakfast fare like omelets and also rotate in some seasonal specials, so there's usually something a little different to try if you're a regular. It's the kind of place where you know what you're getting and it's just good, reliable food.

Just keep in mind they close at 2pm every day, so this is strictly a breakfast and lunch spot. Plan accordingly.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
SATE is the kind of spot you want to tell your friends about. It's on Roswell Road and the owner is genuinely passionate about Vietnamese food in a way that makes the whole experience feel different from your average takeout place. He'll actually talk to you about the food if you're curious, and people say that makes it memorable.

The pho is the real draw here. Reviewers say they make it the authentic way, and even people trying pho for the first time have walked out as fans. It's a great weekday lunch stop or an easy dinner when you don't want anything heavy.

Prices are on the lower end, which makes it an easy yes any night of the week. They're open every day from 10am to 9pm, so it's one of the more flexible options in the area.
address: 5980 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
Royal Spice is a good Indian restaurant on Hammond Drive that hits the right notes for a dinner out. The staff is friendly and actually helpful when you have questions about the menu, which matters a lot if you're newer to Indian food or want to know what's what before you order. It's a nice sit-down experience without being stuffy.

The entrees are the main event here, and reviewers say the appetizers are worth trying too. It's a solid choice for a weeknight dinner or a low-key date night when you want something flavorful and a little different.

Note that they close between lunch and dinner most days, reopening at 4:30pm. So it's not the place to pop in at 3pm on a Tuesday.
address: 220 Hammond Dr #302, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Sunnyside Pizzeria is genuinely one of the best pizza spots in the Sandy Springs area, and the 1,000-plus reviews back that up. It's a great call for a casual family dinner or a Friday night when you just want really good pizza without a long drive. The inside has a neighborhood pizzeria feel, relaxed and easy.

The Hot Honey Charred pizza is what people keep coming back for, and the Hot Honey Lemon Pepper Chicken Wings are worth ordering as a starter. The Caesar Salad is also a solid choice if you want something simple and fresh on the side. Reviewers say the wings alone are reason enough to visit.

They're closed on Mondays, so keep that in mind if pizza night falls on a Monday at your house.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir Suite 149, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is the spot we call when it's a pizza night and nobody wants to argue about where to order. It's on Roswell Road in a strip mall, nothing fancy, but the pizza is legitimately good and the prices are fair. Works great for a weeknight dinner or feeding a group without breaking the bank.

If you haven't tried the stuffed pizza yet, that's where you need to start. Reviews say one slice is basically a full meal, so go in with that expectation. The regular pies are solid too and they deliver, which is always a win on a Friday night.

Just know they're closed on Tuesdays, and Sunday hours start at noon.
address: 2211 Roswell Rd suite 116, Marietta, GA 30062, USA
